combat_history.event: Cheng Kung class frigates conducted patrol operations during Chinese missile tests and military exercises near Taiwan. ROCS Cheng Kung and Chi Kuang maintained heightened readiness posture.
combat_history.significance: First operational deployment during major military crisis, demonstrated Taiwan's commitment to maintaining sea lane security

combat_history.event: Multiple Cheng Kung class frigates conducted extended patrols in South China Sea approaches, intercepting suspected smuggling vessels and conducting freedom of navigation operations.
combat_history.significance: Validated long-range patrol capability and interoperability with allied maritime forces

combat_history.event: Increased operational tempo with daily patrols during heightened cross-strait tensions. Multiple vessels have conducted electronic intelligence gathering missions tracking PLAN vessel movements.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated sustained operational capability under current threat environment

modernization.description: Comprehensive upgrade including replacement of Mk 15 Phalanx with SeaRAM systems, upgraded electronic warfare suite, improved radar processing, and enhanced communications systems.
modernization.impact: Significantly improved point defense capability against anti-ship missiles and enhanced survivability

modernization.description: Potential replacement of Mk 13 launcher with VLS cells, integration of indigenous Hsiung Feng III missiles, and upgraded fire control systems.
modernization.impact: Would provide modern multi-mission capability comparable to contemporary frigates

vulnerabilities.description: Single-arm Mk 13 launcher provides limited missile engagement rate compared to modern VLS systems. Cannot engage multiple simultaneous threats effectively.
vulnerabilities.context: Critical weakness against China's saturation attack tactics using multiple anti-ship missiles
vulnerabilities.mitigation: SeaRAM upgrades provide some improvement but fundamental limitation remains

vulnerabilities.description: Limited electronic warfare and jamming resistance compared to modern Chinese systems. Older combat systems vulnerable to cyber warfare attacks.
vulnerabilities.context: China's advanced EW capabilities could degrade sensors and communications during conflict
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Ongoing upgrades to EW suite but significant capability gap remains

vulnerabilities.description: 30-year-old hull design with limited space and power for modern systems upgrades. Maintenance requirements increasing with age.
vulnerabilities.context: Structural limitations constrain modernization options as threat environment evolves
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Taiwan developing next-generation frigate design but timeline extends beyond 2030

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Taiwan Strait patrol, convoy escort, and maritime domain awareness missions
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates independently or with Kang Ding class frigates and Fast Attack Craft
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo has stressed maintenance cycles; availability rates estimated at 65-70%

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 054A features 32-cell VLS system vs single-arm launcher, modern integrated combat system, and superior electronic warfare capabilities. Cheng Kung class retains ASW advantage with towed array capability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: Taiwan's aging frigates vs China's modern fleet

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Cheng Kung retained full ASW capability that US removed from later Perry class vessels. Similar armament and sensors but Taiwan versions have improved damage control systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: How Taiwan improved on the proven Perry design for local requirements

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Formidable class features VLS cells and more modern combat systems but smaller size. Both designed for regional defense missions with Western weapons integration.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Small nation naval strategies: Singapore vs Taiwan frigate approaches

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar displacement and mission but Anzac class features VLS systems and more advanced radars. Both emphasize ASW capability and regional patrol missions.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Allied frigate designs: How different nations approached similar requirements

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Russian design emphasizes land attack capability with Kalibr missiles while Cheng Kung focuses on air defense and ASW. Similar vintage but different tactical approaches.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East vs West frigate philosophy: Russian power projection vs Taiwanese sea denial

images.title: ROCN Cheng Ho (PFG2-1103) Shipped at Zuoying Naval Base 20141123.jpg
images.description: ​拉起全艦飾、停泊於左營軍港西碼頭的成功級巡防艦鄭和（PFG2-1103），攝於2014年左營軍港營區開放活動。後方白色建物為海軍一二四艦隊部。
